nodes.name: Azure-rumped parrot
nodes.summary: Azure-rumped parrot（Tanygnathus sumatranus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Azure-rumped parrot。

nodes.name: Azure-rumped parrot
nodes.summary: The azure-rumped parrot (Tanygnathus sumatranus) is a large bird endemic to Indonesia. It is found in Sulawesi and the Sangir Islands. It was previously conspecific with the blue-backed parrot, which is differentiated with its red iris and blue back versus the azure-rumped parrot's yellow iris and plain back. It is found in forests. Flocks are small and often active at night. Its main threats are habitat loss and trapping for the pet trade. Taxonomy There are two subspecies: Tanygnathus sumatranus sumatranus (Raffles, 1822): Sulawesi. Sulawesi and nearby islands. Yellow iris. Tanygnathus sumatranus sangirensis Meyer, AB & Wiglesworth, 1894: Sangir Islands and Karakelong. More blue on wing bends and wing coverts, head darker green. Yellow iris Previously, only sumatranus was recognized, but sangirensis was also recognized by the International Ornithological Congress in 2022 based on phylogenetic evidence. Description It is of medium size (32 cm), primarily green with yellowish edging to the wings, a blue rump, and blue wing bends. The head, mantle, wings and tail are darker green, the belly and collar are lighter green. It is sexually dimorphic, with the male having a red beak and the female a pale yellow or horn colored beak. References Juniper & Parr (1998) Parrots: A Guide to Parrots of the World; ISBN 0-300-07453-0. External links Oriental Bird Images: Azure-rumped parrot Selected photos
nodes.shortSummary: The azure-rumped parrot (Tanygnathus sumatranus) is a large bird endemic to Indonesia. It is found in Sulawesi and the Sangir Islands.

nodes.name: Tanygnathus
nodes.summary: Tanygnathus（Tanygnathus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联5种鸟类，如Black-lored parrot、Great-billed parrot、Blue-naped parrot等5种鸟类。
